name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: A code review record exists for the change (e.g., pull/merge request with review comments or approval).
The code review was completed BEFORE the change was merged into the main branch (review timestamp precedes merge timestamp).
The change was not merged to main without an associated code review record.

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: The change has at least one recorded approval on the pull/merge request.
The approver is a different individual from the author/committer of the change (4-eyes principle).
The approver did not author any of the commits included in the change under review.
The approval was granted prior to merge to the main branch.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Unit tests achieve minimum 80% code coverage for new code.
Integration tests achieve minimum 60% coverage for critical integration points.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN 100% coverage is achieved for those critical paths.
IF the change modifies legacy code THEN existing coverage levels are maintained or improved.
Branch coverage is at least 70%.
Function coverage is at least 80%.
A coverage report was generated and reviewed as part of the code review.
All unit tests passed with zero failures prior to release.
All integration tests passed prior to release.
End-to-end tests for critical user journeys passed prior to release.
IF performance tests are applicable THEN performance benchmarks were met.
Security scan results were reviewed and approved prior to release.
Tests were executed in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results are visible in the pull request status checks.
Tests ran in a clean environment with no cached dependencies.
No flaky tests remained unresolved at the time of release (fixed or removed).

Pre-release checklist items are verified: all automated tests pass, coverage meets thresholds, no known critical/high-severity bugs, performance tests pass (if applicable), security tests pass, and manual smoke tests for critical features are completed.

IF an exception/waiver is claimed THEN the change falls within one of the documented exception categories (legacy integration, third-party dependencies, PoC/experimental, documentation-only, build/deploy scripts, dependency version bumps, or refactoring with no behavioral changes) and the exception is documented.
